nameMeanings.name: Dame
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Informal short form of   David  , sometimes bestowed as an independent given name.

variants.name: David
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: Dame Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel was born in 1305, in Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, France. She married Lord/Seigneur Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel in 1325, in Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, France. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Dame Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel est née en 1305, à Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, France. Elle a épousé Lord/Seigneur Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel en 1325, à Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Dame Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel wurde 1305 in Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, Frankreich geboren. Sie hat 1325 in Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, Frankreich Lord/Seigneur Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Dame Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel è nata il nel 1305 nel Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, Francia. Lei sposato Lord/Seigneur Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel nel 1325, nel Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ko.lifeSummary: Dame Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel(은)는 1305년 프랑스 피카디 아이스니 브루이e레스-수-프e르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1325년 프랑스 피카디 아이스니 브루이e레스-수-프e르에서 Lord/Seigneur Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Dame Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel nasceu em 1305, em Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, França. Ela casou-se com Lord/Seigneur Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel em 1325, em Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Dame Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel родилась в 1305 г. в местности бруиeрес-сур-фeр, эисн, пикарди, Франция. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord/Seigneur Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel в 1325 г. в местности бруиeрес-сур-фeр, эисн, пикарди, Франция. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Dame Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel nació en 1305, en Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, Francia. Se casó con Lord/Seigneur Thomas de Bruyères-le-Châtel en 1325, en Bruyères-sur-Fère, Aisne, Picardie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
